description.txt: A workspace Owner using Soleur as their AI organization. Workspaces may have MULTIPLE Owners (ADR-038, ADR-073 team workspaces); Owner-shared surfaces (e.g. the operator email-triage inbox, ADR-066) are readable by every Owner, not just one founder.
Vendors, regulators, and counsel emailing the operator address. Their statutory/operational mail is the email-triage inbox's inbound source.

A beta tester or prospect the operator is in sales/product conversations with. The origin of the third-party contact + conversation PII captured in the owner-private beta-CRM (beta_contacts / interview_notes). An involuntary data subject (Art. 14); no direct access to the store.

An untrusted contributor opening a pull request. Their PR-head code is executed only by the fix-constraints Stage A producer (pull_request, contents:read, no write token) — never by the privileged Stage B consumer, which opens a data-only draft follow-up PR from the default branch (ADR-074).

Inbound email ingress (operator-inbox webhook, svix-verified) + transactional outbound (statutory triage notifications). ADR-066.

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats — NOT git-data, whose beat is unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate (#6548), and NOT registry-liveness, which is paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured — and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

Keyless container-image signing/verification (cosign/Fulcio/Rekor). The release CI keyless-signs the pushed web app image digest (this IS a live sigstore call — github → sigstore below). The host then cosign-verifies the signature FULLY OFFLINE against a pinned trusted_root.json — it makes NO live sigstore call at verify time (ADR-087, #6005) — supply-chain authenticity for the shared host (#5933 Item 4).

The connected repo's own committed plugins/soleur/ dir, present in the user's workspace when they connect a Soleur-dogfooding repo. UNTRUSTED: its hooks/hooks.json command-hooks would run in the Node dispatch process (outside bwrap). INERT for the SDK — both real-SDK factories load plugins:[{path: getPluginPath()}] (the platform-deployed root), never this copy (ADR-093 trust boundary).

platform.infra.tunnel.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel, exactly ONE connector — the designated ingress host, web-1)

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). INVARIANT (ADR-114 I1, enforced #6425): exactly ONE connector is registered — only the designated ingress host (web-1) runs cloudflared (server.tf web_tunnel_connector); other web hosts install the binary and register nothing. This is what makes `localhost:` ingress mean 'this host'. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo, so a SECOND connector silently makes every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6416. The invariant is NOT tied to ADR-068 §(c) (LB weight); re-pooling needs full infra-config + SSH re-delivery (#6466), never a weight flip. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

platform.infra.tunnel.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)

Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.technology: PostgreSQL — dedicated EU project, Supavisor session pooler :5432

description.txt: Inngest config + run history. Dedicated project isolates connection budget + fate from the main app DB (#5450). Public tables RLS-locked; reachable only as the postgres owner (ADR-030 I8)

platform.infra.inngestPostgres.title: Supabase PostgreSQL (Inngest)

description.txt: Inngest durable queue + run-state. AOF on the persistent volume survives a host re-provision — the armed-reminder durability fix (#5450)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)
Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

platform.infra.gitDataStore.title: Shared git-data

description.txt: Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

1bs2yhc.title: deploy. → localhost:9000 (webhook) and ssh. → localhost:22 on the DESIGNATED INGRESS HOST. `localhost:` is CONNECTOR-relative — the ADR-114 I2 anti-pattern — so this edge lands on whichever host runs cloudflared. Contrast the registry. edge, which is genuinely origin-relative (10.0.1.30). /hooks/deploy then fans out to peers over the private net (ADR-068 Option B); deploy-status, inngest-liveness and infra-config do NOT fan out, so they answer from the connector host alone.

11cm60i.technology: cloudflared access tcp → raw TCP over WebSocket (tcp://, NOT http:// — #6122)

wrysao.title: CI reaches the deny-all-public zot host to dual-push release images, and reaches web-1's shell for the 12 terraform_data SSH provisioners — both ONLY through this tunnel, via CF Access service tokens (separate tokens per route, so registry-write rotates independently of host-shell access)
wrysao.technology: cloudflared access tcp/ssh (CF Access service token)

1fz0cwj.title: ONLY the designated ingress host (web-1) runs a cloudflared connector — gated by server.tf's web_tunnel_connector (#6425). 1f7921l.title: Injects secrets — dedicated host's boot credential is scoped to the SEPARATE Doppler project `soleur-inngest` (its own `prd` root holds only inngest secrets: Postgres URI, freshly-rotated signing/event keys, Redis pw), NOT a `prd` branch config that would inherit all ~116 soleur/prd secrets (ADR-100, #6178; mirrors the soleur-registry isolation #6122)

1e38ta8.title: Ships disk-state observability — df% of /var/lib/zot + resize2fs before/after + zot health — as one SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK event, plus a SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC private-NIC convergence event (#6415), to the isolated Logs source 2457081; token in soleur-registry/prd, queried via betterstack-query.sh --grep SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK|SOLEUR_PRIVATE_NIC (#6244/#6415). ALSO pushes two distinct absence-based heartbeats, whose scopes must not be conflated: the 900s disk beat pings on df alone, so it alarms HOST death but stays green with zot dead; the 60s liveness beat (zot-liveness-heartbeat.timer, #6537) pings ONLY while zot answers on the host's own PRIVATE IP — never loopback, since zot binds 0.0.0.0 and loopback answers on a NIC-less host (#6400). The liveness beat's monitor stays PAUSED until #6537's post-merge reprovision measures a real beat and arms it; consumer-perspective reachability is unbuilt (#6438 §1)

Those 3 are the third substrate: Inngest-scheduled but GHA-executed, so their check-in rides this edge, not webapp's. Of 49 cron monitors, 6 check in from here and 43 from webapp. apply-sentry-infra.yml also auto-applies the sentry root on push to main via the jianyuan/sentry provider (ADR-031 — no dashboard step; its `paths:` filter is the 3 rule files, not the whole root)
jeai27.technology: HTTPS (cron check-in API + Terraform provider, SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN)

1svrnoq.title: Pages the operator — the LARGER of two independent exits (betterstack is the other; a Sentry outage is survivable BY DESIGN). Issue alerts route actions_v2 notify_email → target_type IssueOwners with fallthrough_type ActiveMembers — 21 of the 22 rules; `byok_cap_exceeded` deliberately sets fallthrough_type NoOne. NB IssueOwners has no ownership rule on this project, so the fallthrough is what actually reaches a human: a NoOne rule fires and pages nobody, by design there and a defect anywhere else. The cron monitors open an issue on a missed/failed check-in (failure_issue_threshold = 1) and the uptime monitors on downtime, both of which then fire that same email route

padqwg.title: Pages the operator INDEPENDENTLY of Sentry — the second source. The apex uptime monitor and the inngest + registry-disk heartbeats alert on downtime/missed beats (the git-data heartbeat does NOT — it is unfed and absent live, #6548; the registry LIVENESS heartbeat does not yet either — its feeder ships in #6537 and it is armed only once a real beat is measured post-reprovision); on the free tier this is email to the managed recipient, escalating via betteruptime_policy when var.betterstack_paid_tier is set. This is the exit that survives a Sentry-side outage, which is the entire reason the vendor is duplicated
padqwg.technology: Better Stack monitor/heartbeat → email (+ policy escalation on paid tier)

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). INVARIANT (ADR-114 I1, enforced #6425): exactly ONE connector is registered — only the designated ingress host (web-1) runs cloudflared (server.tf web_tunnel_connector); other web hosts install the binary and register nothing. This is what makes `localhost:` ingress mean 'this host'. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo, so a SECOND connector silently makes every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6416. The invariant is NOT tied to ADR-068 §(c) (LB weight); re-pooling needs full infra-config + SSH re-delivery (#6466), never a weight flip. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

nodes.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel, exactly ONE connector — the designated ingress host, web-1)

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats — NOT git-data, whose beat is unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate (#6548), and NOT registry-liveness, which is paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured — and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

External uptime/heartbeat monitoring (BetterStackHQ/better-uptime) AND a Logs warehouse (source 2457081, ClickHouse-SQL-queryable) that CI polls read-only. Apex + inngest + registry-disk heartbeats — NOT git-data, whose beat is unfed (no probe cron was ever written) and absent from live Better Stack despite having no count gate (#6548), and NOT registry-liveness, which is paused until #6537's feeder ships and a beat is measured — and a per-host origin absence detector that probes each web host's CF-proxied hostname (web-N.app.soleur.ai/health) directly, so a dead/never-booted host pages with per-host attribution the round-robin monitors cannot give (#5933 Item 1). The Logs source receives SOLEUR_ZOT_DISK self-reports from the deny-all registry host and is polled from GitHub Actions via betterstack-query.sh for the zot restart-loop recurrence alarm (#6291) + follow-through soak probes — log-content recurrence alarms are in-repo GH-cron pollers, not native Better Stack alerts (ADR-096).

nodes.title: Cloudflare Tunnel

description.txt: Zero-trust inbound access — no exposed ports. ONE tunnel (tunnel.tf) with THREE ingress rules (deploy./ssh./registry.). INVARIANT (ADR-114 I1, enforced #6425): exactly ONE connector is registered — only the designated ingress host (web-1) runs cloudflared (server.tf web_tunnel_connector); other web hosts install the binary and register nothing. This is what makes `localhost:` ingress mean 'this host'. CF binds ingress to a TUNNEL and picks a connector per edge colo, so a SECOND connector silently makes every non-fan-out route (deploy-status, inngest-liveness, infra-config WRITEs, ssh.) answer from whichever replica the caller's colo selected — #6425 (16h of false inngest-down alarms), #6416. The invariant is NOT tied to ADR-068 §(c) (LB weight); re-pooling needs full infra-config + SSH re-delivery (#6466), never a weight flip. Standing guard: the */15 connector census in scheduled-inngest-health.yml (vantage-independent; a response-poll is colo-sticky and proves nothing). Management plane only: app.soleur.ai does NOT traverse the tunnel (direct CF-proxied A record, dns.tf); no cloudflared on zot/git-data/inngest (ADR-096).

nodes.technology: cloudflared (ONE tunnel, exactly ONE connector — the designated ingress host, web-1)

description.txt: Severity-ranked operational-notification store (ADR-085, #6007). Mutable read/act/archive state, workspace-grain, Owner-shared reads — SEPARATE from the email-triage WORM statutory ledger. Content-minimized (title + source_ref ids only). GET /api/inbox merges it with email-triage into one severity-ordered feed (statutory pinned first).

Owner-private beta-tester / prospect CRM (ADR-102, #6165). Third-party contact PII + dual-lens conversation notes + append-only stage-transition history. Owner-only RLS; writes RPC-only via auth.uid()-pinned SECURITY DEFINER functions; the cro/cpo agents read AND write via the crm_* MCP tools (agent-native parity). 24-month retention; Art. 17 via ON DELETE CASCADE + crm_erase_contact.

Self-hosted EU Redis — ADR-059 replay buffer + session/control state; network-reachable, per-workspace key namespacing, TLS + requirepass. DISTINCT node from the loopback Inngest Redis (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

nodes.title: Shared git-data

description.txt: Shared bare git repos (objects/refs) over the private net; per-user worktrees stay host-local on NVMe. The git-data host holds the per-ref monotonic max generation and writer-side-CAS-rejects stale lease writes (ADR-068)

nodes.technology: Bare git repos over private net

description.txt: Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

Co-located stateless sticky router (in each web-host process, not a separate box) — resolves a session's owning host from the per-user worktree lease at the WS-upgrade handshake; local ⇒ serve, remote ⇒ proxy over one-way-TLS private net. Control ops are host-local (no cross-host forwarding). Lease in Postgres (ADR-068 user-sticky amendment)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)

Durable trigger layer for server-side agents — cron, oneshot, and HTTP-armed reminders. Extracted to its own single-host private-net node so exactly-one-scheduler is enforced by topology, unblocking active-active web (ADR-030, ADR-100, #6178)

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.

description.txt: Placement, health-reschedule, and rolling deploys across the Hetzner cluster (ADR-068 Phase 4a)

Cluster of Hetzner Cloud hosts (Nomad clients, spread placement) running web app + CLI engine containers; per-user worktrees on host-local NVMe (ADR-068). At first boot a fresh host pulls the web app image from GHCR and extracts its baked bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl via docker cp (ADR-080, #5921)

PRIVATE GHCR registry (ghcr.io/jikig-ai/soleur-web-platform + soleur-inngest-bootstrap) — the ORIGINAL host pull source, now the DUAL-PUSH + break-glass FALLBACK during the migration to self-hosted zot (ADR-096, #6122); retired from the pull path after the Phase-5 soak (App tokens can't pull GHCR, so it cannot deliver a zero-touch machine credential — ADR-088 superseded). Still bakes the host bootstrap scripts + hooks.json.tmpl, extracted via docker cp at first boot to keep cloud-init user_data under Hetzner's 32,768-byte cap (ADR-080, #5921).

OCI-native container registry (zot) on a dedicated Hetzner host (cx33, 4 vCPU / 8 GB, hel1, volume-backed) on the private network (10.0.1.30:5000), behind a deny-all-public firewall — the zero-touch machine-credential registry that replaces GHCR on the pull path (ADR-096, #6122). Sized cx33 in hel1 (#6288 — the plan's cx32 target does not exist as a Hetzner type; cx33 is the real 8 GB type, offered in hel1 not nbg1) with an ADR-062 --memory=7168m cap so zot's ~35 GB store boot scan cannot host-OOM restart-loop the box. The 60 GB store volume is a disposable GHCR mirror: the nbg1→hel1 migration recreates it fresh and it re-fills from GHCR on the next CI dual-push. Hosts pull the SAME @sha256 digests here (crane-copy from CI preserves them); cosign verifies the zot-stored .sig offline against the unchanged pinned root. Plain-HTTP on the private net (integrity via cosign digest-pinning, not TLS); dark-launch gated with an atomic GHCR fallback until the Phase-5 soak retires GHCR push.

Error tracking + alerting, Sentry-as-IaC (ADR-031; apps/web-platform/infra/sentry/, auto-applied on push to main by apply-sentry-infra.yml — its `paths:` filter is issue-alerts.tf/cron-monitors.tf/uptime-monitors.tf, so versions/main/variables changes do NOT auto-apply). Org `jikigai-eu` on the EU/DE cluster. TWO distinct hostname classes: the Terraform/dashboard API is the ORG SUBDOMAIN (`https://<org>.sentry.io/api/`, see main.tf's provider block — NOT `eu.sentry.io`, whose regional host rewrites `-eu`-suffixed slugs to the literal `eu` org via an activeorg-cookie hijack, 302→401, breaking every read/write); DSN ingest is `de.sentry.io` (DE residency, Art. 30 PA8 §(e)). Holds the LARGER of the two paging surfaces: the issue alerts (issue-alerts.tf), the cron monitors (cron-monitors.tf — one per scheduled workflow, `failure_issue_threshold` opens an issue on a missed check-in) and the uptime monitors (uptime-monitors.tf). It is a no-SSH paging path — a host that never finished booting still pages through here. It is deliberately NOT the only one: `betterstack` is a SECOND-SOURCE vendor that pages independently precisely so a Sentry outage is survivable (see its own edge to founder). Do not 'consolidate' the two — the redundancy is the design.
Claude Code instances executing agent workflows
